situation: audit-2026-07-06-022 H-003 finding: _fetch_page_text(url) at line 197 calls urllib.request.urlopen directly on untrusted URL from Bing SERP results with no scheme/IP/redirect validation. Attacker-controlled search result URLs could trigger SSRF to file://, gopher://, internal IPs (169.254.169.254 metadata, RFC1918), or localhost services.
action: Added _validate_fetch_url() helper that checks: (1) scheme must be http/https, (2) hostname resolves to non-private/non-loopback/non-link-local IP via socket.getaddrinfo + ipaddress module, (3) known metadata hostnames blocked. Added _NoPrivateRedirectHandler subclass of HTTPRedirectHandler that blocks post-redirect SSRF by validating redirect targets. Replaced bare urlopen with _opener.open using the safe handler. All SSRF vectors tested and rejected; legitimate public URLs pass.
outcome_evidence: Commit cb4ca49 pushed to origin/auto/opt-wechat-main-1783130541. Python syntax check passed. SSRF validation unit tests passed: file://, gopher://, 127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, 10.x, 192.168.x all rejected; https://www.example.com and http://news.sina.com.cn accepted.
next_suggestion: Dispatch H-004: fact_checker.py:322 empty search auto-FALSIFIED → change to UNVERIFIABLE + manual review
Dispatch H-005: qq_push.py:42 hardcoded /opt/wanxia/.env no permission check → stat mode verify

audit_findings.finding: SSRF via _fetch_page_text — urllib.request.urlopen called on untrusted URL without scheme/IP/redirect validation
audit_findings.remediation: Add _validate_fetch_url (scheme whitelist + DNS resolve + private IP block) + _NoPrivateRedirectHandler for post-redirect check; use safe opener instead of bare urlopen
